- deep-research: Deep research harness — fan-out web searches, fetch sources, adversarially verify claims, synthesize a cited report. - When the user wants a deep, multi-source, fact-checked research report on any topic. BEFORE invoking, check if the question is specific enough to research directly — if underspecified (e.g., "what car to buy" without budget/use-case/region), ask 2-3 clarifying questions to narrow scope. Then pass the refined question as args, weaving the answers in.
- artifact-design: Design guidance and fundamentals for Artifacts.
- update-config: Use this skill to configure the Claude Code harness via settings.json. Automated behaviors ("from now on when X", "each time X", "whenever X", "before/after X") require hooks configured in settings.json - the harness executes these, not Claude, so memory/preferences cannot fulfill them. Also use for: permissions ("allow X", "add permission", "move permission to"), env vars ("set X=Y"), hook troubleshooting, or any changes to settings.json/settings.local.json files. Examples: "allow npm commands", "add bq permission to global settings", "move permission to user settings", "set DEBUG=true", "when claude stops show X". For simple settings like theme/model, suggest the /config command.
- keybindings-help: Use when the user wants to customize keyboard shortcuts, rebind keys, add chord bindings, or modify ~/.claude/keybindings.json. Examples: "rebind ctrl+s", "add a chord shortcut", "change the submit key", "customize keybindings".
- verify: Verify that a code change actually does what it's supposed to by running the app and observing behavior. Use when asked to verify a PR, confirm a fix works, test a change manually, check that a feature works, or validate local changes before pushing.
- code-review: Review the current diff for correctness bugs and reuse/simplification/efficiency cleanups at the given effort level (low/medium: fewer, high-confidence findings; high→max: broader coverage, may include uncertain findings; ultra: deep multi-agent review in the cloud). Pass --comment to post findings as inline PR comments, or --fix to apply the findings to the working tree after the review.
- simplify: Review the changed code for reuse, simplification, efficiency, and altitude cleanups, then apply the fixes. Quality only — it does not hunt for bugs; use /code-review for that.
- fewer-permission-prompts: Scan your transcripts for common read-only Bash and MCP tool calls, then add a prioritized allowlist to project .claude/settings.json to reduce permission prompts.
- loop: Run a prompt or slash command on a recurring interval (e.g. /loop 5m /foo). Omit the interval to let the model self-pace. - When the user wants to set up a recurring task, poll for status, or run something repeatedly on an interval (e.g. "check the deploy every 5 minutes", "keep running /babysit-prs"). Do NOT invoke for one-off tasks.
- schedule: Create, update, list, or run scheduled cloud agents (routines) that execute on a cron schedule. - When the user wants to schedule a recurring cloud agent, set up automated tasks, create a cron job for Claude Code, or manage their scheduled agents/routines. Also use when the user wants a one-time scheduled run ("run this once at 3pm", "remind me to check X tomorrow").
- claude-api: Reference for the Claude API / Anthropic SDK — model ids, pricing, params, streaming, tool use, MCP, agents, caching, token counting, model migration.
TRIGGER — read BEFORE opening the target file; don't skip because it "looks like a one-liner" — whenever: the prompt names Claude/Anthropic in any form (Claude, Anthropic, Fable, Opus, Sonnet, Haiku, `anthropic`, `@anthropic-ai`, `claude-*`, `us.anthropic.*`, `[1m]`); the user asks about an LLM (pricing/model choice/limits/caching) — never answer from memory; OR the task is LLM-shaped with provider unstated (agent/MCP/tool-definition/multi-agent/RAG/LLM-judge/computer-use; generate/summarize/extract/classify/rewrite/converse over NL; debugging refusals/cutoffs/streaming/tool-calls/tokens).
SKIP only when another provider is being worked on (overrides all triggers): OpenAI/GPT/Gemini/Llama/Mistral/Cohere/Ollama named in the query; OR `grep -rE 'openai|langchain_openai|google.generativeai|genai|mistralai|cohere|ollama'` over the project hits (run this grep FIRST if no provider named — don't Read the file).
- run: Launch and drive this project's app to see a change working. Use when asked to run, start, or screenshot the app, or to confirm a change works in the real app (not just tests). First looks for a project skill that already covers launching the app; otherwise falls back to built-in patterns per project type (CLI, server, TUI, Electron, browser-driven, library).
- init: Initialize a new CLAUDE.md file with codebase documentation
- review: Review a GitHub pull request; for your working diff use /code-review
- security-review: Complete a security review of the pending changes on the current branch

410	def _perceptual_hash(img_bytes: bytes, hash_size: int = 16) -> str:
411	    """Compute a perceptual hash (16x16 grayscale average hash) of an image."""
412	    img = Image.open(io.BytesIO(img_bytes)).convert("L").resize(
413	        (hash_size, hash_size), Image.LANCZOS,
414	    )
415	    pixels = list(img.getdata())
416	    avg = sum(pixels) / len(pixels)
417	    bits = "".join("1" if p > avg else "0" for p in pixels)
418	    # Convert binary string to hex
419	    return hex(int(bits, 2))[2:].zfill(hash_size * hash_size // 4)
420	
421	
422	def _pixel_diff(
423	    img_a_bytes: bytes, img_b_bytes: bytes, tolerance: int = CHANNEL_TOLERANCE,
424	) -> tuple[float, Optional[bytes]]:
425	    """Compare two images pixel-by-pixel with channel tolerance.
426	
427	    Returns (diff_ratio, diff_image_png_bytes_or_None).
428	    """
429	    img_a = Image.open(io.BytesIO(img_a_bytes)).convert("RGBA")
430	    img_b = Image.open(io.BytesIO(img_b_bytes)).convert("RGBA")
431	
432	    if img_a.size != img_b.size:
433	        return 1.0, None
434	
435	    w, h = img_a.size
436	    px_a = img_a.load()
437	    px_b = img_b.load()
438	    total = w * h
439	    diff_count = 0
440	    diff_img = Image.new("RGBA", (w, h), (0, 0, 0, 0))
441	    diff_px = diff_img.load()
442	
443	    for y in range(h):
444	        for x in range(w):
445	            ra, ga, ba, aa = px_a[x, y]
446	            rb, gb, bb, ab = px_b[x, y]
447	            if (
448	                abs(ra - rb) > tolerance
449	                or abs(ga - gb) > tolerance
450	                or abs(ba - bb) > tolerance
451	                or abs(aa - ab) > tolerance
452	            ):
453	                diff_count += 1
454	                diff_px[x, y] = (255, 0, 0, 180)  # Red overlay
455	            else:
456	                # Dimmed original
457	                diff_px[x, y] = (ra // 3, ga // 3, ba // 3, 255)
458	
459	    ratio = diff_count / total if total > 0 else 0.0
460	    buf = io.BytesIO()
461	    diff_img.save(buf, format="PNG")
462	    return ratio, buf.getvalue()
463

465	def _img_to_base64(img_bytes: bytes) -> str:
466	    """Convert PNG bytes to a data URI for embedding in HTML."""
467	    b64 = base64.b64encode(img_bytes).decode("ascii")
468	    return f"data:image/png;base64,{b64}"

475	class ServerManager:
476	    """Manage the live_server.py subprocess."""
477	
478	    def __init__(self, port: int):
479	        self.port = port
480	        self.process: Optional[subprocess.Popen] = None
481	        self.log_path = OUTPUT_DIR / "server.log"
482	
483	    async def start(self) -> None:
484	        """Start the live server and wait for it to be healthy."""
485	        log.info("Starting live_server.py on port %d ...", self.port)
486	        log_file = open(self.log_path, "w")
487	        self.process = subprocess.Popen(
488	            [
489	                sys.executable, "-u",
490	                str(_PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts" / "live_server.py"),
491	                "--port", str(self.port),
492	            ],
493	            stdout=log_file,
494	            stderr=subprocess.STDOUT,
495	            cwd=str(_PROJECT_ROOT),
496	        )
497	        # Wait for server to respond
498	        import aiohttp
499	        url = f"http://localhost:{self.port}/health"
500	        for attempt in range(30):
501	            await asyncio.sleep(1)
502	            try:
503	                async with aiohttp.ClientSession() as session:
504	                    async with session.get(url, timeout=aiohttp.ClientTimeout(total=3)) as resp:
505	                        if resp.status == 200:
506	                            log.info("Server healthy after %d seconds", attempt + 1)
507	                            return
508	            except Exception:
509	                pass
510	            if self.process.poll() is not None:
511	                raise RuntimeError(
512	                    f"Server process exited with code {self.process.returncode}. "
513	                    f"Check {self.log_path}"
514	                )
515	        raise RuntimeError(f"Server did not become healthy within 30 seconds. Check {self.log_path}")
516	
517	    async def stop(self) -> None:
518	        """Stop the server process."""
519	        if self.process and self.process.poll() is None:
520	            log.info("Stopping server (PID %d) ...", self.process.pid)
521	            self.process.terminate()
522	            try:
523	                self.process.wait(timeout=5)
524	            except subprocess.TimeoutExpired:
525	                self.process.kill()
526	                self.process.wait(timeout=3)
527	            log.info("Server stopped.")

586	def _make_route_handler(resp: dict[str, Any]):
587	    """Factory to create a route handler with properly captured response data.
588	
589	    Playwright route callbacks receive (route, request) as two positional args.
590	    A naive lambda ``lambda route, resp=response: ...`` would have ``resp``
591	    overwritten by the ``request`` positional arg.  This factory avoids that.
592	    """
593	    async def handler(route):
594	        await route.fulfill(
595	            status=resp["status"],
596	            content_type=resp["content_type"],
597	            body=resp["body"],
598	        )
599	    return handler

755	async def element_clip_screenshot(
756	    page: Page, selector: str, padding: int = 15,
757	) -> Optional[bytes]:
758	    """Screenshot an element via expanded bounding-box clip (Trap 2 fix).
759	
760	    Returns None if the element is off-screen, invisible, or has zero area.
761	    """
762	    el = page.locator(selector).first
763	    if await el.count() == 0:
764	        return None
765	    try:
766	        box = await el.bounding_box()
767	    except Exception:
768	        return None
769	    if not box or box["width"] <= 0 or box["height"] <= 0:
770	        return None
771	
772	    vp = page.viewport_size or {"width": 1440, "height": 900}
773	
774	    # Compute clip with padding, clamped to viewport
775	    x = max(0, box["x"] - padding)
776	    y = max(0, box["y"] - padding)
777	    w = min(box["width"] + 2 * padding, vp["width"] - x)
778	    h = min(box["height"] + 2 * padding, vp["height"] - y)
779	
780	    # Skip if element is off-screen or clip would be empty
781	    if w <= 0 or h <= 0 or x >= vp["width"] or y >= vp["height"]:
782	        return None
783	
784	    try:
785	        return await page.screenshot(clip={"x": x, "y": y, "width": w, "height": h})
786	    except Exception:
787	        return None  # Graceful fallback for edge cases

794	async def section_a_navigation(
795	    page: Page, base_url: str,
796	) -> dict[str, Any]:
797	    """Section A: Navigate all 15 states, capture screenshots, verify uniqueness."""
798	    log.info("=== SECTION A: Navigation + Uniqueness ===")
799	    results: dict[str, Any] = {
800	        "section": "A_navigation",
801	        "states_tested": 0,
802	        "screenshots_captured": 0,
803	        "unique_states": 0,
804	        "duplicate_pairs": [],
805	        "state_details": [],
806	        "pass": False,
807	    }
808	
809	    hashes: dict[str, list[str]] = defaultdict(list)
810	    captured = 0
811	
812	    for theme in THEMES:
813	        for vp_name in BASE_VIEWPORTS:
814	            vp = VIEWPORTS[vp_name]
815	            await page.set_viewport_size(vp)
816	
817	            # Load page ONCE per theme+viewport (avoids 60 CDN round-trips)
818	            await safe_goto(page, base_url)
819	            await apply_frozen_values(page)
820	            await inject_deterministic_result(page)
821	            await page.evaluate(f"""
822	                document.documentElement.setAttribute('data-theme', '{theme}');
823	                localStorage.setItem('polaris-theme', '{theme}');
824	            """)
825	            await page.wait_for_timeout(200)
826	
827	            for state_entry in STATE_MATRIX:
828	                # Switch state via JS (no page reload)
829	                await navigate_to_state(page, state_entry)
830	
831	                # Capture screenshot
832	                fname = f"{state_entry['name']}_{theme}_{vp_name}.png"
833	                fpath = SCREENSHOTS_DIR / fname
834	                img_bytes = await full_page_screenshot(page, fpath)
835	                captured += 1
836	                log.info("  [A] %s (%d/%d)", fname, captured, len(STATE_MATRIX) * len(THEMES) * len(BASE_VIEWPORTS))
837	
838	                # Compute perceptual hash
839	                phash = _perceptual_hash(img_bytes)
840	                hash_key = f"{theme}_{vp_name}"
841	                hashes[hash_key].append((state_entry["name"], phash, img_bytes))
842	
843	                results["state_details"].append({
844	                    "state": state_entry["name"],
845	                    "theme": theme,
846	                    "viewport": vp_name,
847	                    "file": fname,
848	                    "phash": phash,
849	                    "size_bytes": len(img_bytes),
850	                })
851	
852	    results["screenshots_captured"] = captured
853	    results["states_tested"] = len(STATE_MATRIX)
854	
855	    # Check for duplicate hashes within same theme+viewport
856	    # When hashes match, confirm with pixel_diff to eliminate false positives
857	    # (pHash at 16×16 can produce identical hashes for visually distinct states)
858	    duplicates = []
859	    unique_count = 0
860	    for key, hash_list in hashes.items():
861	        seen: dict[str, tuple[str, bytes]] = {}
862	        for name, phash, img_data in hash_list:
863	            if phash in seen:
864	                prev_name, prev_img = seen[phash]
865	                diff_ratio, _ = _pixel_diff(prev_img, img_data)
866	                if diff_ratio < 0.01:
867	                    # Less than 1% pixel difference → truly duplicate
868	                    duplicates.append({
869	                        "context": key,
870	                        "state_a": prev_name,
871	                        "state_b": name,
872	                        "phash": phash,
873	                        "pixel_diff": round(diff_ratio, 4),
874	                    })
875	                else:
876	                    # pHash false positive — images are visually different
877	                    log.info(
878	                        "  [A] pHash match but %.1f%% pixel diff: %s vs %s (%s) — not duplicate",
879	                        diff_ratio * 100, prev_name, name, key,
880	                    )
881	                    unique_count += 1
882	            else:
883	                seen[phash] = (name, img_data)
884	                unique_count += 1
885	
886	    results["unique_states"] = unique_count
887	    results["duplicate_pairs"] = duplicates
888	    results["pass"] = len(duplicates) == 0
889	
890	    log.info(
891	        "Section A: %d screenshots, %d unique, %d duplicates",
892	        captured, unique_count, len(duplicates),
893	    )
894	    return results

1204	async def _compute_element_key(page: Page, locator: Any) -> str:
1205	    """Return a stable CSS path string for dedup across views."""
1206	    try:
1207	        return await locator.evaluate("""el => {
1208	            const parts = [];
1209	            let node = el;
1210	            while (node && node !== document.body) {
1211	                let selector = node.tagName.toLowerCase();
1212	                if (node.id) {
1213	                    selector += '#' + node.id;
1214	                    parts.unshift(selector);
1215	                    break;
1216	                }
1217	                const parent = node.parentElement;
1218	                if (parent) {
1219	                    const siblings = Array.from(parent.children).filter(c => c.tagName === node.tagName);
1220	                    if (siblings.length > 1) {
1221	                        selector += ':nth-of-type(' + (siblings.indexOf(node) + 1) + ')';
1222	                    }
1223	                }
1224	                parts.unshift(selector);
1225	                node = node.parentElement;
1226	            }
1227	            return parts.join(' > ');
1228	        }""")
1229	    except Exception:
1230	        return ""

1976	# SECTION J: CSS Hardcoded Value Scan (Static Analysis)
1977	# =========================================================================
1978	
1979	def section_j_css_scan() -> dict[str, Any]:
1980	    """Section J: Scan CSS files for hardcoded values."""
1981	    log.info("=== SECTION J: CSS Hardcoded Value Scan ===")
1982	    results: dict[str, Any] = {
1983	        "section": "J_css_scan",
1984	        "hardcoded_font_sizes": [],
1985	        "hardcoded_hex_colors": [],
1986	        "hardcoded_z_index": [],
1987	        "duplicate_variables": [],
1988	        "total_issues": 0,
1989	        "pass": False,
1990	    }
1991	
1992	    for css_file in CSS_FILES:
1993	        fpath = CSS_DIR / css_file
1994	        if not fpath.exists():
1995	            log.warning("CSS file not found: %s", fpath)
1996	            continue
1997	
1998	        content = fpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
1999	        lines = content.split("\n")
2000	
2001	        in_root = False
2002	        in_print = False
2003	        root_depth = 0
2004	
2005	        for line_num, line in enumerate(lines, 1):
2006	            stripped = line.strip()
2007	
2008	            # Track :root blocks (variables declared here are OK)
2009	            if ":root" in stripped:
2010	                in_root = True
2011	                root_depth = 0
2012	            if "@media print" in stripped:
2013	                in_print = True
2014	
2015	            if in_root or in_print:
2016	                root_depth += stripped.count("{") - stripped.count("}")
2017	                if root_depth <= 0:
2018	                    in_root = False
2019	                    in_print = False
2020	                continue
2021	
2022	            # font-size with px (not in :root or @media print)
2023	            if re.search(r"font-size:\s*\d+px", stripped):
2024	                match = re.search(r"font-size:\s*(\d+px)", stripped)
2025	                results["hardcoded_font_sizes"].append({
2026	                    "file": css_file,
2027	                    "line": line_num,
2028	                    "value": match.group(1) if match else "?",
2029	                    "context": stripped[:100],
2030	                })
2031	
2032	            # z-index not using var(--z-*)
2033	            if re.search(r"z-index:\s*\d+", stripped) and "var(--z-" not in stripped:
2034	                match = re.search(r"z-index:\s*(\d+)", stripped)
2035	                results["hardcoded_z_index"].append({
2036	                    "file": css_file,
2037	                    "line": line_num,
2038	                    "value": match.group(1) if match else "?",
2039	                    "context": stripped[:100],
2040	                })
2041	
2042	    # Duplicate variable detection (re-scan :root blocks specifically)
2043	    for css_file in CSS_FILES:
2044	        fpath = CSS_DIR / css_file
2045	        if not fpath.exists():
2046	            continue
2047	        content = fpath.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
2048	        # Find all variable declarations within same selector block
2049	        var_declarations: dict[str, list[tuple[int, str]]] = defaultdict(list)
2050	        current_selector = ""
2051	        for line_num, line in enumerate(content.split("\n"), 1):
2052	            stripped = line.strip()
2053	            # Track selector
2054	            if "{" in stripped and "}" not in stripped:
2055	                current_selector = stripped.replace("{", "").strip()
2056	            elif "}" in stripped:
2057	                # Check for duplicates within this block
2058	                for var_name, decls in var_declarations.items():
2059	                    if len(decls) > 1:
2060	                        results["duplicate_variables"].append({
2061	                            "file": css_file,
2062	                            "variable": var_name,
2063	                            "selector": current_selector,
2064	                            "declarations": [
2065	                                {"line": ln, "value": val} for ln, val in decls
2066	                            ],
2067	                        })
2068	                var_declarations.clear()
2069	                current_selector = ""
2070

1	"""
2	visual_review_gate.py — UI harness Action Authorization Boundary.
3	
4	Implements the AAB pattern (Faramesh arxiv 2601.17744) + Ralph-Wiggum
5	loop (Vercel 2025) + Visual Validator Tool (Anthropic harness 2025) for
6	POLARIS v6 UI PRs.
7	
8	Contract:
9	- Input:  PR head branch with v6 UI changes; routes-under-test list.
10	- Output: `.codex/<issue_id>/codex_visual_audit.txt` with machine-parseable
11	  YAML per `.codex/visual_audit_rubric.md`. Final `verdict: APPROVE` line
12	  is what the codex-visual-required CI gate parses (last-match wins, per
13	  the PR-D iter-2 hardening pattern already used by codex-required.yml).
14	- State: persisted per-Issue at `.codex/<issue_id>/visual_iter_state.json`
15	  so the iter counter survives across writer edits. Force-APPROVE fires
16	  ONLY when state.iter == 5 — `--max-iter` is informational, never an
17	  early-force lever (P0-iter1 fix: bind force-APPROVE to absolute iter 5
18	  not to user-supplied flag).
19	- Halt: returns non-zero exit code if any route fails the 14/16 threshold
20	  AND iter < 5; force-APPROVE at exact iter 5 per CLAUDE.md §8.3.1.
21	
22	Why this lives OUTSIDE the writer agent's reasoning loop:
23	- The script's loop condition is the YAML pass_count, not the writer's
24	  judgment. The writer cannot "decide it's good enough" and skip the
25	  validator — the next-iter trigger is mechanical.
26	- The CI workflow `codex-visual-required` runs this script on the PR
27	  branch deterministically. The writer cannot bypass by editing prompts.
28	- Combined with codex-required.yml's `canonical-diff-sha256` binding,
29	  the visual gate cannot be approved on a different page than the one
30	  in the PR. The audit declares `pr_head_sha` + `screenshots_manifest_sha256`
31	  which CI cross-binds.
32	
33	Usage:
34	    python scripts/visual_review_gate.py \\
35	        --issue-id I-ux-002 \\
36	        --routes /inspector/test-run-001,/intake,/plan,/dashboard \\
37	        --base-url http://127.0.0.1:3000
38	
39	Environment:
40	    NEXT_PROD_BASE_URL  — defaults to http://127.0.0.1:3000; the script
41	                          does NOT start the server (the caller does;
42	                          must be `next start` of a production build,
43	                          NOT `next dev`, because Playwright Chromium
44	                          on Windows fails dev-mode WS handshake).
45	    PG_VISUAL_GATE_VIEWPORTS — comma list of WxH (default
46	                          1440x900,768x1024,390x844).
47	    CODEX_BIN           — defaults to `codex` on PATH.
48	
49	This script is intentionally Pythonic (cross-platform) — `subprocess`
50	calls into `codex exec` mirror the existing brief-review workflow
51	(env -u OPENAI_API_KEY codex exec --skip-git-repo-check -i <png>).
52	"""
53	
54	from __future__ import annotations
55	
56	import argparse
57	import asyncio
58	import hashlib
59	import json
60	import os
61	import re
62	import shutil
63	import subprocess
64	import sys

117	class CodexOutputError(RuntimeError):
118	    """Raised when Codex returns malformed or incomplete YAML.
119	
120	    Codex diff-iter-1 P1 fix: invalid Codex output must NOT silently
121	    advance the iter counter and reach iter-5 force-APPROVE. Hard error
122	    instead. The harness fails closed; the writer must re-run.
123	    """
124	
125	
126	@dataclass
127	class CodexVerdict:
128	    raw: str
129	    verdict: str | None = None
130	    pass_count: int | None = None
131	    rubric_sha256_declared: str | None = None
132	    screenshot_sha256_declared: str | None = None
133	
134	    @classmethod
135	    def parse(cls, raw: str, label: str = "<unknown>") -> "CodexVerdict":
136	        """Strict parser. Raises CodexOutputError on any missing field
137	        or out-of-range value. Iter-state save sites MUST catch and
138	        treat as hard harness failure, not as REQUEST_CHANGES.
139	        """
140	        v = cls(raw=raw)
141	        m = FINAL_VERDICT_RE.findall(raw)
142	        v.verdict = m[-1] if m else None
143	        if v.verdict not in ("APPROVE", "REQUEST_CHANGES"):
144	            raise CodexOutputError(
145	                f"{label}: missing or invalid `verdict:` line "
146	                f"(got {v.verdict!r}); Codex returned malformed output"
147	            )
148	
149	        m = PASS_COUNT_RE.findall(raw)
150	        v.pass_count = int(m[-1]) if m else None
151	        if v.pass_count is None:
152	            raise CodexOutputError(
153	                f"{label}: missing `pass_count:` line"
154	            )
155	        if not (0 <= v.pass_count <= 16):
156	            raise CodexOutputError(
157	                f"{label}: pass_count={v.pass_count} out of range 0..16"
158	            )
159	
160	        m = RUBRIC_SHA_RE.findall(raw)
161	        v.rubric_sha256_declared = m[-1] if m else None
162	        if not v.rubric_sha256_declared:
163	            raise CodexOutputError(
164	                f"{label}: missing `rubric_sha256:` declaration"
165	            )
166	
167	        m = SCREENSHOT_SHA_RE.findall(raw)
168	        v.screenshot_sha256_declared = m[-1] if m else None
169	        if not v.screenshot_sha256_declared:
170	            raise CodexOutputError(
171	                f"{label}: missing `screenshot_sha256:` declaration"
172	            )
173	
174	        return v
175	
176	    def is_approve(self) -> bool:
177	        return self.verdict == "APPROVE"

191	def sha256_file(path: Path) -> str:
192	    h = hashlib.sha256()
193	    with path.open("rb") as f:
194	        for chunk in iter(lambda: f.read(65536), b""):
195	            h.update(chunk)
196	    return h.hexdigest()
197	
198	
199	def sha256_text(text: str) -> str:
200	    return hashlib.sha256(text.encode("utf-8")).hexdigest()
201	
202	
203	def sha256_bytes(blob: bytes) -> str:
204	    return hashlib.sha256(blob).hexdigest()

225	def ui_surface_tree_sha() -> str:
226	    """Compute a stable hash of the UI surface tracked by the gate.
227	
228	    Codex diff-iter-1 P0 fix: the previous design recorded
229	    `pr_head_sha = git rev-parse HEAD` in the audit. But the audit
230	    file must then be COMMITTED, which changes HEAD, and the CI gate
231	    compared declared `pr_head_sha` to `github.event.pull_request.head.sha`
232	    — an impossible fixed point.
233	
234	    The fix: bind the audit to the UI surface CONTENT (web/app/** +
235	    web/components/**), not the commit identity. This hash is invariant
236	    across the audit-commit step because the audit-commit does not touch
237	    those paths.
238	
239	    Implementation is delegated to `scripts/compute_ui_surface_sha.py`
240	    so the script and the CI workflow share one source of truth for
241	    the algorithm.
242	    """
243	    # Local import to avoid cycle at module load if compute_ui_surface_sha
244	    # is itself being introspected.
245	    sys.path.insert(0, str(PROJECT_ROOT / "scripts"))
246	    try:
247	        import compute_ui_surface_sha  # type: ignore
248	        return compute_ui_surface_sha.compute(PROJECT_ROOT)
249	    finally:
250	        sys.path.pop(0)

281	# Screenshot pass (Playwright async)
282	# Captures THREE states per route+viewport so the rubric's interaction
283	# dimensions (13 motion, 14 keyboard/focus, partially 16 liveliness) are
284	# observable from the harness inputs:
285	#   - static   : page as-loaded, animations disabled
286	#   - focused  : first interactive given keyboard focus (Tab)
287	#   - hovered  : first interactive given mouse hover
288	# (P1-iter1 fix: rubric dim 13/14/16 evidence path.)
289	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
290	async def capture_screenshots(
291	    routes: list[str],
292	    base_url: str,
293	    viewports: list[tuple[int, int]],
294	    outdir: Path,
295	) -> list[ScreenshotJob]:
296	    try:
297	        from playwright.async_api import async_playwright
298	    except ImportError as exc:
299	        raise SystemExit(
300	            "playwright not installed. Run: pip install playwright && playwright install chromium"
301	        ) from exc
302	
303	    outdir.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
304	    jobs: list[ScreenshotJob] = []
305	
306	    async with async_playwright() as pw:
307	        # Production build only — Chromium dev-mode WS handshake fails on
308	        # Windows per `feedback_*` notes. Caller starts `next start`.
309	        browser = await pw.chromium.launch(
310	            headless=True,
311	            args=["--disable-dev-shm-usage", "--no-sandbox"],
312	        )
313	        try:
314	            for route in routes:
315	                for vw in viewports:
316	                    context = await browser.new_context(
317	                        viewport={"width": vw[0], "height": vw[1]},
318	                        device_scale_factor=2,
319	                    )
320	                    page = await context.new_page()
321	                    url = base_url.rstrip("/") + route
322	                    print(f"[shot] {url} @ {vw[0]}x{vw[1]}", flush=True)
323	                    try:
324	                        await page.goto(url, wait_until="networkidle", timeout=30_000)
325	                    except Exception as exc:
326	                        print(f"  WARN: navigation failed: {exc}", flush=True)
327	                    await page.wait_for_timeout(500)
328	
329	                    # static
330	                    static_job = ScreenshotJob(
331	                        route=route,
332	                        viewport=vw,
333	                        state="static",
334	                        output_path=outdir / "",
335	                    )
336	                    static_job.output_path = outdir / f"{static_job.label}.png"
337	                    await page.screenshot(
338	                        path=str(static_job.output_path),
339	                        full_page=True,
340	                        animations="disabled",
341	                    )
342	                    jobs.append(static_job)
343	
344	                    # focused — Tab to first interactive, screenshot focus ring
345	                    try:
346	                        await page.keyboard.press("Tab")
347	                        await page.wait_for_timeout(150)
348	                    except Exception as exc:
349	                        print(f"  WARN: focus pass failed: {exc}", flush=True)
350	                    focused_job = ScreenshotJob(
351	                        route=route,
352	                        viewport=vw,
353	                        state="focused",
354	                        output_path=outdir / "",
355	                    )
356	                    focused_job.output_path = outdir / f"{focused_job.label}.png"
357	                    await page.screenshot(
358	                        path=str(focused_job.output_path),
359	                        full_page=False,  # viewport only — focus ring is in viewport
360	                    )
361	                    jobs.append(focused_job)
362	
363	                    # hovered — hover first link/button if present, screenshot
364	                    try:
365	                        candidate = page.locator("a, button").first
366	                        if await candidate.count() > 0:
367	                            await candidate.hover(timeout=2_000)
368	                            await page.wait_for_timeout(150)
369	                    except Exception as exc:
370	                        print(f"  WARN: hover pass failed: {exc}", flush=True)
371	                    hovered_job = ScreenshotJob(
372	                        route=route,
373	                        viewport=vw,
374	                        state="hovered",
375	                        output_path=outdir / "",
376	                    )
377	                    hovered_job.output_path = outdir / f"{hovered_job.label}.png"
378	                    await page.screenshot(
379	                        path=str(hovered_job.output_path),
380	                        full_page=False,
381	                    )
382	                    jobs.append(hovered_job)
383	
384	                    await context.close()
385	        finally:
386	            await browser.close()
387	    return jobs

390	def build_screenshots_manifest(jobs: list[ScreenshotJob]) -> str:
391	    """Deterministic JSON inventory of captured screenshots.
392	
393	    Lists each screenshot with its content SHA256. The audit declares
394	    a single `screenshots_manifest_sha256:` line; the CI gate verifies
395	    that hash matches the screenshots directory currently in the PR.
396	    (P0-iter1 fix: bind audit to actual rendered evidence.)
397	    """
398	    entries = []
399	    for job in sorted(jobs, key=lambda j: j.label):
400	        entries.append(
401	            {
402	                "label": job.label,
403	                "route": job.route,
404	                "viewport": list(job.viewport),
405	                "state": job.state,
406	                "sha256": sha256_file(job.output_path),
407	            }
408	        )
409	    return json.dumps(entries, indent=2, sort_keys=True)

413	# Codex visual-audit call
414	# ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
415	PROMPT_TEMPLATE = textwrap.dedent("""\
416	HARD ITERATION CAP: 5 per document. This is iter {iter_n} of 5.
417	- Front-load ALL real findings in iter 1. No drip-feeding across iterations.
418	- Same quality bar regardless of iteration count.
419	- "Don't pick bone from egg" — if a finding isn't a real solid blocker, classify it as P3/P2/cosmetic; reserve P0/P1 for real execution risks.
420	- If iter 5 returns REQUEST_CHANGES, the document is force-APPROVE'd by Claude on remaining-non-P0/P1 findings; do not bank issues for iter 6.
421	- If you detect "I'm holding back a P1 to surface in the next round" — DON'T. Surface it now. The 5-cap means iter 6 doesn't exist; banked findings die at iter 5.
422	- Verdict APPROVE iff zero NOVEL P0 AND zero continuing P0 AND zero P1.
423	
424	TASK
425	====
426	
427	You are reviewing a screenshot of a POLARIS v6 UI route against the
428	locked 16-dimension visual rubric.
429	
430	ROUTE        : {route}
431	VIEWPORT     : {viewport_w}x{viewport_h}
432	STATE        : {state}   # static / focused / hovered
433	SCREENSHOT   : attached via -i flag
434	RUBRIC PATH  : .codex/visual_audit_rubric.md
435	RUBRIC SHA   : {rubric_sha}
436	SCREENSHOT SHA: {screenshot_sha}
437	
438	RUBRIC (verbatim, do NOT paraphrase dimension labels):
439	
440	{rubric_body}
441	
442	INSTRUCTIONS
443	============
444	
445	1. Score each of the 16 dimensions PASS / PARTIAL / FAIL with ONE
446	   sentence of evidence quoting the pixel region or visible token.
447	2. For "focused" screenshots, weight dim 14 (keyboard+focus) primary
448	   evidence. For "hovered" screenshots, weight dim 13 (motion). For
449	   "static" screenshots, weight dims 1–12.
450	3. Count PASS scores into `pass_count` (0..16). Threshold = 14.
451	4. Emit ONLY the YAML block specified in the rubric — no surrounding
452	   prose. The harness parses the LAST `verdict:` line as authoritative.
453	5. `rubric_sha256` MUST equal `{rubric_sha}` (the gate enforces this).
454	6. `screenshot_sha256` MUST equal `{screenshot_sha}` (the gate enforces this).
455	7. PARTIAL counts as NOT PASS for `pass_count`.
456	
457	Begin YAML now:
458	""")
459	
460	
461	def run_codex_audit(
462	    job: ScreenshotJob, iter_n: int, rubric_sha: str, rubric_body: str
463	) -> CodexVerdict:
464	    screenshot_sha = sha256_file(job.output_path)
465	    prompt = PROMPT_TEMPLATE.format(
466	        iter_n=iter_n,
467	        route=job.route,
468	        viewport_w=job.viewport[0],
469	        viewport_h=job.viewport[1],
470	        state=job.state,
471	        rubric_sha=rubric_sha,
472	        rubric_body=rubric_body,
473	        screenshot_sha=screenshot_sha,
474	    )
475	    env = {k: v for k, v in os.environ.items() if k != "OPENAI_API_KEY"}
476	    cmd = [
477	        codex_bin(),
478	        "exec",
479	        "--skip-git-repo-check",
480	        "-i",
481	        str(job.output_path),
482	        "-",
483	    ]
484	    print(f"[codex] iter={iter_n} {job.label}", flush=True)
485	    proc = subprocess.run(
486	        cmd,
487	        input=prompt,
488	        text=True,
489	        capture_output=True,
490	        env=env,
491	        timeout=540,
492	        check=False,
493	    )
494	    if proc.returncode != 0:
495	        print(f"  codex stderr: {proc.stderr[:500]}", flush=True)
496	    # Strict parse — raises CodexOutputError on malformed output. The
497	    # caller does NOT catch + treat as REQUEST_CHANGES; it propagates
498	    # to main() which exits without advancing iter state.
499	    return CodexVerdict.parse(proc.stdout, label=job.label)
500

505	def emit_verdict_file(
506	    issue_id: str,
507	    iter_n: int,
508	    iter_result: IterResult,
509	    rubric_sha: str,
510	    ui_surface_tree_sha256: str,
511	    screenshots_manifest_sha: str,
512	    force_approved: bool,
513	) -> Path:
514	    target = PROJECT_ROOT / ".codex" / issue_id / "codex_visual_audit.txt"
515	    target.parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
516	
517	    lines: list[str] = []
518	    lines.append(f"# POLARIS visual gate — issue {issue_id}, iter {iter_n}")
519	    lines.append(f"# rubric: .codex/visual_audit_rubric.md")
520	    lines.append(f"# threshold: {PASS_THRESHOLD}/16 per route+viewport+state")
521	    lines.append("")
522	    # Audit-to-PR cross-binding declarations — CI gate verifies each.
523	    lines.append(f"rubric_sha256: {rubric_sha}")
524	    lines.append(f"ui_surface_tree_sha256: {ui_surface_tree_sha256}")
525	    lines.append(f"screenshots_manifest_sha256: {screenshots_manifest_sha}")
526	    lines.append("")
527	    for label, v in iter_result.per_job.items():
528	        lines.append(f"## {label}")
529	        lines.append(v.raw.strip())
530	        lines.append("")
531	    lines.append("# ---- aggregate ----")
532	    lines.append(f"iter: {iter_n}")
533	    lines.append(f"min_pass_count: {iter_result.min_pass_count}")
534	    lines.append(f"all_approve: {iter_result.all_approve}")
535	    if force_approved:
536	        lines.append(
537	            f"# force-approved at iter {iter_n} cap per CLAUDE.md §8.3.1"
538	        )
539	        lines.append("verdict: APPROVE")
540	    elif iter_result.all_approve:
541	        lines.append("verdict: APPROVE")
542	    else:
543	        lines.append("verdict: REQUEST_CHANGES")
544	    target.write_text("\n".join(lines), encoding="utf-8")
545	    return target

582	def main() -> int:
583	    args = parse_args()
584	
585	    if args.reset_state:
586	        reset_iter_state(args.issue_id)
587	        print(f"reset visual_iter_state.json for {args.issue_id}")
588	
589	    if not RUBRIC_PATH.exists():
590	        print(f"ERROR: rubric not found at {RUBRIC_PATH}", file=sys.stderr)
591	        return 2
592	    rubric_body = RUBRIC_PATH.read_text(encoding="utf-8")
593	    rubric_sha = sha256_text(rubric_body)
594	    print(f"rubric_sha256: {rubric_sha}", flush=True)
595	
596	    routes = [r.strip() for r in args.routes.split(",") if r.strip()]
597	    if not routes:
598	        print("ERROR: --routes is empty", file=sys.stderr)
599	        return 2
600	
601	    viewports = viewport_list()
602	    print(f"viewports: {viewports}", flush=True)
603	
604	    # Persisted state — the absolute 5-iter cap lives here. (P0 fix.)
605	    # Iter counter is INCREMENTED-AND-SAVED only AFTER a successful
606	    # Codex iteration (P1 from diff iter-1): malformed Codex output
607	    # MUST NOT advance the iter counter, otherwise repeated garbage
608	    # responses reach iter-5 force-APPROVE without a single valid
609	    # review.
610	    state = load_iter_state(args.issue_id)
611	    iter_n = state.get("iter", 0) + 1  # tentative — saved only on success
612	    if iter_n > HARD_ITER_CAP:
613	        # Already force-approved on a prior call; the audit file should
614	        # exist. Re-running is a no-op.
615	        print(
616	            f"iter {iter_n} > HARD_ITER_CAP={HARD_ITER_CAP}; "
617	            f"force-APPROVE has already fired. Use --reset-state to start over.",
618	            flush=True,
619	        )
620	        return 0
621	    print(f"\n=== iter {iter_n}/{HARD_ITER_CAP} (tentative; saved on success) ===", flush=True)
622	
623	    ui_surface_tree_sha256 = ui_surface_tree_sha()
624	    print(f"ui_surface_tree_sha256: {ui_surface_tree_sha256}", flush=True)
625	
626	    screenshots_root = Path(args.screenshots_dir) / args.issue_id / f"iter_{iter_n}"
627	    if screenshots_root.exists():
628	        shutil.rmtree(screenshots_root)
629	    screenshots_root.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
630	
631	    if not shutil.which(codex_bin()) and not args.no_codex:
632	        print(
633	            f"ERROR: codex CLI not on PATH (`{codex_bin()}`). Install per "

1	"""Plan-sufficiency gate — I-meta-005 Phase 3 (#987). The money-trap fix.
2	
3	`assess_plan_sufficiency` decides whether the BILLED evidence set covers EVERY
4	planned sub-question to its per-section evidence target at the numeric authority
5	floor — BEFORE a generator token is billed. It is a PURE function (no network,
6	no LLM) over rows that already carry the §2.3a authority sidecar + `query_origin`
7	provenance, plus the pinned `ResearchPlan`.
8	
9	KEY DESIGN (brief §2.2):
10	- Coverage UNIT = the section outline item (its `evidence_target`).
11	- Relevance = PROVENANCE-FIRST: a row is relevant to a section iff its
12	  `query_origin` matches (normalized equality) one of the sub-query texts at the
13	  section's `sub_query_indices`. Two content-word overlap fallbacks exist, both
14	  reusing the SAME deterministic `_content_words` + `MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP=2`
15	  primitive (NO embedding router, §8.4):
16	  - SENTINEL/EMPTY origin (`{primary_trial_doi_seed, agentic_seed, deepener_seed,
17	    need_type_backend, domain_backend}` or `""`): these lanes surface
18	    authoritative evidence with NO originating sub-query, so their relevance is
19	    decided by overlapping the ROW CONTENT (statement + direct_quote) against the
20	    section's sub-query texts.
21	  - REAL-ORIGIN DRIFT FALLBACK (A5, RC6): a row whose `query_origin` is a REAL
22	    sub-query text but whose EXACT-string match yields EMPTY (e.g. a STORM
23	    expansion drift that no longer string-equals the planned sub-query) falls
24	    THROUGH to the SAME row-content overlap path as the sentinel/empty branch —
25	    overlapping the ROW CONTENT (statement + direct_quote) against the section's
26	    sub-query texts. This recovers a drifted origin's orphaned evidence; a row
27	    with no usable content (or no overlap) stays orphaned. The fallback fires
28	    ONLY when exact-match is empty — it never AUGMENTS a non-empty exact match.
29	    Gated by `PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_ORIGIN_DRIFT_FALLBACK` (default ON; set to 0
30	    for the pre-A5 strict-exact behavior). Routing only: it changes WHICH section
31	    sees a row, never WHETHER its sentences verify.
32	- Authority floor = the NUMERIC `authority_score` (float [0,1]); a row counts
33	  toward coverage iff `authority_score >= authority_floor` (a single global
34	  float, NOT a per-domain dict). Below-floor relevant rows are reported, not
35	  credited.
36	- Section SUFFICIENT iff BOTH (facet-level, not section-aggregate):
37	    (i)  total above-floor covered_count >= evidence_target, AND
38	    (ii) EVERY mapped sub_query_index has >= MIN_PER_FACET above-floor relevant
39	         rows — so a section mapped to [4,5,6] cannot pass on rows from 4 alone.
40	- Verdict: PROCEED (all sufficient) / EXPAND (≥1 under-covered, round < max) /
41	  ABORT (≥1 under-covered, rounds/budget exhausted).
42	
43	The relevance MAPPING is shared with the generator's on-mode
44	`_assign_evidence_to_planned_outline` via `relevant_section_indices`, so a
45	section certified SUFFICIENT actually RECEIVES its credited rows (brief §2.2b).
46	
47	NO `if domain ==` / NO `_DEFAULT_DOMAIN_THRESHOLDS` / NO clinical literal on this
48	path — sufficiency is computed from the PLAN x AUTHORITY, never a domain.
49	"""
50	from __future__ import annotations
51	
52	import logging
53	import os
54	from dataclasses import dataclass, field
55	from typing import Any, Literal
56	
57	logger = logging.getLogger("polaris_graph.plan_sufficiency_gate")
58	
59	SufficiencyVerdict = Literal["proceed", "expand", "abort"]
60	
61	# NON-QUERY sentinel origins that legitimately carry no sub-query text (brief
62	# §2.2). Rows with one of these origins (or an empty origin) are FALLBACK-
63	# ELIGIBLE: their relevance is decided by content-word overlap against a
64	# section's sub-query texts. A REAL sub-query origin is authoritative and uses
65	# provenance-first matching only. Codex-LOCKED — do not widen.
66	SENTINEL_ORIGINS: frozenset[str] = frozenset(
67	    # FX-15a (#1118): `agentic_seed` (agentic-discovered URLs) and `deepener_seed` (citation-
68	    # snowball deepener URLs, Codex iter-1 P1) are non-query seed lanes, creditable via the overlap
69	    # fallback exactly as the old mislabel `primary_trial_doi_seed` was — so the relabels preserve
70	    # plan-sufficiency fallback-eligibility (no behavior change).
71	    {"primary_trial_doi_seed", "agentic_seed", "deepener_seed", "need_type_backend", "domain_backend"}
72	)
73	
74	
75	def _authority_floor_default() -> float:
76	    """Single global numeric authority floor in [0,1] (brief §2.2). Read at call
77	    time so tests/operators can override via env; callers pass it explicitly."""
78	    try:
79	        return float(os.getenv("PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_AUTHORITY_FLOOR", "0.3"))
80	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
81	        return 0.3
82	
83	
84	def _min_per_facet_default() -> int:
85	    """Minimum above-floor relevant rows EACH mapped sub-query must have for a
86	    section to be sufficient (brief §2.2, default 1)."""
87	    try:
88	        return max(1, int(os.getenv("PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_MIN_PER_FACET", "1")))
89	    except (TypeError, ValueError):
90	        return 1
91	
92	
93	@dataclass
94	class UnitCoverage:
95	    """Per-section coverage detail (brief §2.1)."""
96	
97	    unit_id: str
98	    title: str
99	    evidence_target: int
100	    sub_query_indices: list[int]
101	    covered_count: int                 # above-floor relevant rows (total)
102	    below_floor_count: int             # relevant-but-below-authority (reported)
103	    sufficient: bool
104	    per_facet_covered: dict[int, int] = field(default_factory=dict)
105	    empty_facets: list[int] = field(default_factory=list)
106	
107	
108	@dataclass
109	class PlanSufficiencyReport:
110	    verdict: SufficiencyVerdict
111	    authority_floor: float
112	    min_per_facet: int
113	    round_index: int
114	    max_rounds: int
115	    per_unit: list[UnitCoverage] = field(default_factory=list)
116	    under_covered_units: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
117	    notes: list[str] = field(default_factory=list)
118	
119	
120	def _normalize_query(text: str) -> str:
121	    """Normalized form for provenance equality (whitespace-collapsed, lower)."""
122	    return " ".join(str(text or "").split()).strip().lower()
123	
124	
125	def _content_words(text: str) -> set[str]:
126	    """Reuse the EXISTING grounding tokenizer (alphabetic, >=3 chars, stopword-
127	    stripped) — the same primitive the provenance verifier uses (brief §2.2).
128	    Imported lazily so this module has no import-time generator dependency."""
129	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
130	        _content_words as _cw,
131	    )
132	    return _cw(text)
133	
134	
135	def _min_content_word_overlap() -> int:
136	    """The EXISTING `MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP` constant (default 2) — no new
137	    magic number for the fallback floor (brief §2.2)."""
138	    from src.polaris_graph.generator.provenance_generator import (
139	        MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP,
140	    )
141	    return MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP
142	
143	
144	def _row_text_for_overlap(row: dict[str, Any]) -> str:
145	    """The row content used for the SENTINEL/EMPTY-origin content-word fallback:
146	    statement + the direct_quote span (NOT just the title — brief §2.2 fallback)."""
147	    return " ".join(
148	        str(row.get(k) or "")
149	        for k in ("statement", "direct_quote")
150	    )
151	
152	
153	def _origin_drift_fallback_enabled() -> bool:
154	    """A5 (RC6): is the real-origin content-word DRIFT fallback active? Default ON
155	    (the §-1.3-correct behavior — recover an orphaned drifted origin rather than
156	    hard-drop it). Set `PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_ORIGIN_DRIFT_FALLBACK=0` for the
157	    pre-A5 strict-exact-only behavior (byte-identical to the legacy path)."""
158	    val = os.getenv("PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_ORIGIN_DRIFT_FALLBACK", "1").strip().lower()
159	    return val not in ("0", "false", "no", "off", "")
160	
161	
162	def relevant_section_indices(
163	    row: dict[str, Any],
164	    outline: list[Any],
165	    sub_queries: list[str],
166	) -> list[int]:
167	    """Map a row to the outline section index(es) it is RELEVANT to (brief §2.2).
168	
169	    SHARED by the gate (counting) and the on-mode generator assignment so the
170	    SUFFICIENT certification carries through to what the generator receives.
171	    Authority is NOT applied here — relevance is provenance/overlap only; the
172	    gate layers the numeric floor on top.
173	
174	    Provenance-first: a non-sentinel, non-empty `query_origin` credits ONLY the
175	    section(s) whose `sub_query_indices` point at that exact sub-query text. If
176	    that exact match is EMPTY, the A5 (RC6) drift fallback overlaps the ORIGIN
177	    TEXT against each section's sub-query texts (recovers a drifted origin; an
178	    unrelated origin stays orphaned); default ON, gated by
179	    `PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_ORIGIN_DRIFT_FALLBACK`. A sentinel/empty origin uses
180	    content-word overlap of the ROW CONTENT against the section's sub-query
181	    texts. Returns the list of matching section indices (possibly several;
182	    possibly none -> orphan, uncredited).
183	    """
184	    origin = _normalize_query(row.get("query_origin", ""))
185	    raw_origin = str(row.get("query_origin", "") or "")
186	    norm_subqueries = [_normalize_query(sq) for sq in sub_queries]
187	
188	    fallback_eligible = (raw_origin == "") or (raw_origin in SENTINEL_ORIGINS)
189	
190	    matches: list[int] = []
191	    if not fallback_eligible:
192	        # PROVENANCE-FIRST: credit only sections whose mapped sub-query texts
193	        # equal this row's real query_origin.
194	        for sec_idx, section in enumerate(outline):
195	            for q_idx in getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or []:
196	                if 0 <= q_idx < len(norm_subqueries) and norm_subqueries[q_idx] == origin:
197	                    matches.append(sec_idx)
198	                    break
199	        if matches or not _origin_drift_fallback_enabled():
200	            return matches
201	        # A5 (RC6) DRIFT FALLBACK: a REAL sub-query origin whose EXACT match is
202	        # empty (origin drift, e.g. a STORM expansion that no longer string-
203	        # equals the planned sub-query) falls through to the SAME content-word
204	        # overlap path the sentinel/empty branch below already uses — overlapping
205	        # the ROW CONTENT (statement + direct_quote) against each section's
206	        # sub-query texts. Fires ONLY when the exact match above produced nothing
207	        # (never augments a non-empty exact match); a row with no usable content
208	        # stays orphaned. Routing only — never relaxes the gate.
209	        # Falls through to the shared fallback block below.
210	
211	    # FALLBACK (empty / sentinel origin, OR a real origin whose exact match was
212	    # empty per the A5 drift fall-through above): content-word overlap of the row
213	    # content against the section's OWN sub-query texts, floored by
214	    # MIN_CONTENT_WORD_OVERLAP.
215	    row_words = _content_words(_row_text_for_overlap(row))
216	    if not row_words:
217	        return matches
218	    floor = _min_content_word_overlap()
219	    for sec_idx, section in enumerate(outline):
220	        section_words: set[str] = set()
221	        for q_idx in getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or []:
222	            if 0 <= q_idx < len(sub_queries):
223	                section_words |= _content_words(sub_queries[q_idx])
224	        if not section_words:
225	            continue
226	        if len(row_words & section_words) >= floor:
227	            matches.append(sec_idx)
228	    return matches
229	
230	
231	def _facets_matched_for_row(
232	    row: dict[str, Any],
233	    section: Any,
234	    sub_queries: list[str],
235	) -> list[int]:
236	    """Which of THIS section's mapped sub_query_indices the row covers (brief
237	    §2.2 facet-level). Provenance-first for a real origin; content-word overlap
238	    of the ROW CONTENT against the SPECIFIC facet's sub-query text for a
239	    sentinel/empty origin. A5 (RC6): a real origin whose EXACT match yields no
240	    facet falls through to that SAME row-content overlap path — fires ONLY when
241	    the exact match is empty, mirroring `relevant_section_indices` (default ON,
242	    `PG_PLAN_SUFFICIENCY_ORIGIN_DRIFT_FALLBACK`). A row with no usable content
243	    stays orphaned. Routing only — never relaxes the gate."""
244	    raw_origin = str(row.get("query_origin", "") or "")
245	    origin = _normalize_query(raw_origin)
246	    fallback_eligible = (raw_origin == "") or (raw_origin in SENTINEL_ORIGINS)
247	    indices = [
248	        q for q in (getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or [])
249	        if 0 <= q < len(sub_queries)
250	    ]
251	    floor = _min_content_word_overlap()
252	    if not fallback_eligible:
253	        exact = [q for q in indices if _normalize_query(sub_queries[q]) == origin]
254	        # The drift fallback fires ONLY for a GENUINELY orphaned origin (no exact
255	        # match against ANY planned sub-query) — mirroring `relevant_section_
256	        # indices`' GLOBAL suppression. If the origin exact-matches some OTHER
257	        # section's facet, this row belongs there; it must NOT content-rescue
258	        # into THIS section, or the gate would credit coverage the generator's
259	        # routing never delivers (gate/router divergence == reopened money-trap).
260	        origin_matches_any = any(
261	            _normalize_query(sq) == origin for sq in sub_queries
262	        )
263	        if exact or origin_matches_any or not _origin_drift_fallback_enabled():
264	            return exact
265	        # A5 drift fall-through: identical row-content overlap as the sentinel
266	        # branch below.
267	    row_words = _content_words(_row_text_for_overlap(row))
268	    if not row_words:
269	        return []
270	    return [
271	        q for q in indices
272	        if len(row_words & _content_words(sub_queries[q])) >= floor
273	    ]
274	
275	
276	def _enrich_authority_if_missing(row: dict[str, Any]) -> float:
277	    """Return the row's numeric `authority_score`, computing it at gate time for
278	    a billed row that lacks the sidecar (post-selection V30 contract / uploaded-
279	    document rows — brief §2.3a). Builds a minimal signals object from the row's
280	    url/title; thin inputs -> honest LOW per the Phase-0a contract (never a
281	    silent blind credit). Does NOT mutate the row's persisted sidecar contract
282	    beyond filling the missing score for THIS assessment."""
283	    val = row.get("authority_score")
284	    if isinstance(val, (int, float)):
285	        return float(val)
286	    # Missing sidecar -> compute directly from the row's surface signals.
287	    from src.polaris_graph.authority.authority_model import score_source_authority
288	    from src.polaris_graph.retrieval.tier_classifier import ClassificationSignals
289	
290	    url = str(row.get("source_url") or row.get("url") or "")
291	    title = str(row.get("statement") or row.get("title") or "")
292	    body = str(row.get("direct_quote") or "")
293	    signals = ClassificationSignals(
294	        url=url,
295	        title=title,
296	        publisher="",
297	        fetched_content_length=len(body),
298	        fetched_body=body,
299	    )
300	    result = score_source_authority(signals)
301	    score = float(result.authority_score)
302	    # Cache onto the row so the shared mapping / telemetry stays consistent.
303	    row["authority_score"] = score
304	    if not row.get("authority_confidence"):
305	        row["authority_confidence"] = result.authority_confidence.value
306	    return score
307	
308	
309	def assess_plan_sufficiency(
310	    *,
311	    plan: Any,
312	    corpus_rows: list[dict[str, Any]],
313	    authority_floor: float | None = None,
314	    round_index: int,
315	    max_rounds: int,
316	    min_per_facet: int | None = None,
317	) -> PlanSufficiencyReport:
318	    """Certify the BILLED evidence set against the pinned plan (brief §2.1).
319	
320	    Args:
321	        plan: the pinned `ResearchPlan` (carries `sub_queries` + `outline` with
322	            per-section `evidence_target` + `sub_query_indices`).
323	        corpus_rows: the FINAL billed rows (`evidence_for_gen`) — each carries
324	            `query_origin` + (for live rows) the authority sidecar; injected
325	            contract/upload rows are enriched here.
326	        authority_floor: numeric floor in [0,1]; default from env.
327	        round_index / max_rounds: saturation-round bookkeeping (EXPAND vs ABORT).
328	        min_per_facet: per-facet minimum above-floor rows; default from env.
329	
330	    Returns a `PlanSufficiencyReport`. PURE / no-network / no-LLM.
331	    """
332	    floor = _authority_floor_default() if authority_floor is None else float(authority_floor)
333	    per_facet_min = _min_per_facet_default() if min_per_facet is None else max(1, int(min_per_facet))
334	
335	    sub_queries = list(getattr(plan, "sub_queries", []) or [])
336	    outline = list(getattr(plan, "outline", []) or [])
337	
338	    per_unit: list[UnitCoverage] = []
339	    under_covered: list[str] = []
340	
341	    for sec_idx, section in enumerate(outline):
342	        unit_id = f"section_{sec_idx}"
343	        title = getattr(section, "title", "") or unit_id
344	        target = int(getattr(section, "evidence_target", 0) or 0)
345	        mapped = [
346	            q for q in (getattr(section, "sub_query_indices", []) or [])
347	            if 0 <= q < len(sub_queries)
348	        ]
349	        covered_count = 0
350	        below_floor_count = 0
351	        per_facet_covered: dict[int, int] = {q: 0 for q in mapped}
352	
353	        for row in corpus_rows:
354	            matched_facets = _facets_matched_for_row(row, section, sub_queries)
355	            if not matched_facets:
356	                continue
357	            score = _enrich_authority_if_missing(row)
358	            if score >= floor:
359	                covered_count += 1
360	                for q in matched_facets:
361	                    per_facet_covered[q] = per_facet_covered.get(q, 0) + 1
362	            else:
363	                below_floor_count += 1
364	
365	        empty_facets = [
366	            q for q in mapped if per_facet_covered.get(q, 0) < per_facet_min
367	        ]
368	        sufficient = (
369	            covered_count >= target
370	            and target >= 1
371	            and len(mapped) >= 1
372	            and not empty_facets
373	        )
374	        unit = UnitCoverage(
375	            unit_id=unit_id,
376	            title=title,
377	            evidence_target=target,
378	            sub_query_indices=list(mapped),
379	            covered_count=covered_count,
380	            below_floor_count=below_floor_count,
381	            sufficient=sufficient,
382	            per_facet_covered=dict(per_facet_covered),
383	            empty_facets=empty_facets,
384	        )
385	        per_unit.append(unit)
386	        if not sufficient:
387	            under_covered.append(unit_id)
388	
389	    if not under_covered:
390	        verdict: SufficiencyVerdict = "proceed"
391	        notes = [f"all {len(per_unit)} planned sections sufficient at floor {floor}"]
392	    elif round_index < max_rounds:
393	        verdict = "expand"
394	        notes = [
395	            f"{len(under_covered)} under-covered unit(s); "
396	            f"round {round_index} < max {max_rounds} -> EXPAND"
397	        ]
398	    else:
399	        verdict = "abort"
400	        notes = [
401	            f"{len(under_covered)} under-covered unit(s); "
402	            f"rounds exhausted (round {round_index} >= max {max_rounds}) -> ABORT"
403	        ]
404	
405	    return PlanSufficiencyReport(
406	        verdict=verdict,
407	        authority_floor=floor,
408	        min_per_facet=per_facet_min,
409	        round_index=round_index,
410	        max_rounds=max_rounds,
411	        per_unit=per_unit,
412	        under_covered_units=under_covered,
413	        notes=notes,
414	    )
